name: Header-spoofed corporate email (auth failures)
evidence:
From: Microsoft Talent Acquisition <talent@microsoft.com>
Reply-To: msft.recruiting2026@gmail.com
Received: from unknown (198.51.100.23) by mx.recipient.com
Authentication-Results: mx.recipient.com; spf=fail smtp.mailfrom=microsoft.com; dmarc=fail
Subject: Interview confirmation - Cloud Support Engineer

Dear Candidate,

Your profile has been shortlisted for the Cloud Support Engineer position. The
interview will be conducted entirely via chat. To proceed with onboarding,
please send a scan of your passport and your bank account details for payroll
registration within 24 hours.

Regards,
Microsoft Talent Team
notes: The sender claims microsoft.com but fails SPF/DMARC and routes replies to a free mailbox — the wording alone looks professional; the headers tell the truth.
